sections.text: GETTING STARTED Quickstart CORE CONCEPTS Live queries Schema & types GETTING STARTED Quickstart Install the CLI, describe your data, and get a typed, reactive database running on your machine. About five minutes from here to a query that updates itself. # Install Epoch is one package and one CLI. Add it to an existing project, or scaffold a new one with the starter — either way you'll have a local database, schema push, and type generation wired up. terminal Copy npm install epoch Starting fresh? The init command sets up the project structure and an example schema for you. terminal Copy npx epoch@latest init # Describe your data A schema is plain TypeScript. Define your tables, their columns, and the indexes you'll query by. This file is the source of truth for the whole stack — your client types are generated from it. epoch/schema.ts Copy import { defineSchema, defineTable, t } from "epoch/schema"; export default defineSchema({ channels: defineTable({ slug: t.string().unique(), name:

sections.text: LEAVE IT RUNNING Every save re-pushes your schema and re-runs codegen in milliseconds. You should never have to restart it or generate types by hand.

styles.fontFamily: "Inter Variable", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

sections.text: NO LOCK-IN The wire protocol and the server are open source under the MIT license. Self-hosting is a first-class path, not a downgrade — the cloud just runs the same binary for you.
